reasoning: Arithmetic intensity is only 0.25 FLOP/byte, far below the 62 FLOP/byte ridge point, so this kernel has very little computation per byte moved. DRAM bandwidth utilization is 98.9% of peak (496 GB/s), which strongly indicates throughput is capped by memory bandwidth rather than compute. High occupancy (94.6%), low register use (16/thread), and perfect global load efficiency rule out occupancy or spill issues; the high long scoreboard stalls are consistent with waiting on heavily utilized memory traffic, not a low-utilization latency-bound case.
